polish.worst_offense: The compass icon inside the draggable circle at time ~4s is decorative emoji/clip-art inside the drawing surface, violating §3 no-chart-junk and §8 anti-pattern #9.
fixes: Remove the compass icon from inside the green circle handle; the circular affordance and teal color alone signal interactivity without decoration.
Add a subtle soft shadow (0 1px 3px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.15)) to both draggable handles (points a and b) to earn depth as per §3 depth rule and improve affordance clarity to a 4.
Ensure the purple dashed vertical line at time 8s (the end handle) has a matching visual weight and shadow treatment as the start handle for symmetry and discoverability.

issues: The manipulated variable appears to be a continuous sweep parameter (accumulationFunction_sweepX) rather than discrete step-by-step navigation as designed. The intended design calls for explicit 'Next Step' and 'Previous Step' buttons with discrete day/hour/minute granularity control, not a continuous slider.

The visual feedback does not show accumulating stacked Riemann rectangles with individual labels and area contributions. Instead, a continuous curve is swept smoothly across the domain, which obscures the discrete cumulative-sum structure central to the pedagogical goal.

No odometer or running total sum notation (A(x) = Σ[Δx·f(x_i)]) is visible in any frame. The design requires explicit causal-chain feedback showing interval width, function height, area contribution, and running total—none of which appear.

The trace strategy of retaining all previous rectangles in a stacked column to show convergence as step size shrinks is absent. Instead, the figure appears to show only the current sweep state, losing the cumulative visual history needed for the 'aha moment' of integral construction.

The variable `accumulationFunction_snapshotCount` controls a continuous visual property (likely curve position or opacity) rather than the discrete step-count intended by the design. The design calls for discrete Riemann rectangles to appear/disappear as steps accumulate; instead, a single smooth curve shifts smoothly across frames.

No Riemann rectangles (vertical strips) are visible in any frame. The design explicitly requires 'thin vertical rectangles' to appear and stack as the snapshot count increases; the current behavior shows only a smooth curve evolution.

The odometer/sum annotation does not update discretely with each step. The design specifies 'the odometer jumps up by that rectangle's area' and displays running total as Σ notation; no such discrete labeled jumps or sum expressions appear.

The trace strategy of 'all previous rectangles remain visible in a stacked column chart' is absent. Instead of accumulated labeled strips persisting left-to-right, a single continuous curve morphs, violating the core visual feedback mechanism for showing cumulative integration.

results.description: On load, the sweep point x starts at the left bound a, with accumulated area near zero - the student must drag to explore
results.visual_check: sweep point is at the left bound, shaded region is minimal, A(x) graph shows point near origin

visual_verdict.observed: The two screenshots appear identical with no visible changes in the sweep point position, shaded region, or any other visual elements.

results.description: The sweep point x can be dragged rightward along the x-axis
results.visual_check: sweep point moves to x=4, shaded region expands from a to x=4

visual_verdict.observed: The sweep point (speedometer icon) moved from approximately x=3 to x=4 on the timeline, and the shaded region expanded accordingly from point a to include the area up to x=4.

results.description: As sweep point x moves right, the accumulated area A(x) increases in the second graph
results.visual_check: shaded region grows, A(x) curve extends rightward showing increased accumulated area

visual_verdict.observed: The two screenshots appear virtually identical with no visible changes to the shaded region, curve, or any other visual elements.

results.description: The start bound a is displayed as inherited from previous sections at a=0
results.visual_check: left bound marker shows at a=0 with muted styling, no grab cursor on the a marker

visual_verdict.observed: The left bound marker (a) at position 0 now displays with muted/desaturated styling and appears to have lost its interactive grab cursor affordance.

results.description: When f(x) > 0, moving x rightward increases A(x)
results.visual_check: accumulated area value increases as sweep point moves through region where curve is positive

visual_verdict.observed: The two screenshots appear identical with no visible changes to the accumulated area value, sweep point position, or any other visual elements.
